text: I think I would just show him/her this video , you say it all, swiftly and succinctly 🙏🏻🔥💚💫

Hi, I have just asked my consultant if all of this was checked in my first surgery when I had an ablation. I had a few pictures back in 2024. I have just had them come back and say the following- Endometriosis outside pelvis is quite rare. Bowels were not involved at the time of laparoscopy, though we will have an update with MRI. Its not possible to check diaphragm completely with laparoscopy due to position of upper abdominal organs.
I have gone back again and asked what was checked and mentioned about wanting a 360 assessment. also they will look to do an excision this time. apparently it wasnt possible the first time because my womb was too angry and inflamed and bleeding. Wondering if what my specialist has said is right or should I be seeking to speak to another specialist? many thanks.
